# Light Burn or Light Stress

**Problem:** Your cannabis plant can only withstand a certain amount of light. After a certain point, your cannabis will start turning yellow or otherwise exhibit signs of stress on the leaves near the sources of light and/or heat.
[How far away do I keep grow lights from my plants?](https://www.growweedeasy.com/how-far-grow-lights)
**Light burn usually causes yellow leaves at the top of the plant directly under the grow lights (though it can appear on older leaves that have been exposed for a long time).**
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2014/04/light-burn-leaf-cannabis.jpg)
Sometimes the **first sign** a plant is getting too much light is all the leaves start pointing up or “praying”, like this (though sometimes you don’t see any symptoms until the yellowing starts)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/05/canopy-of-medical-marijuana-buds-flowering.jpg)
With light burn, often the inside veins stay green. Yellow leaves won’t fall off or be plucked off easily, unlike a nitrogen deficiency where leaves fall off on their own.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/06/yellow-leaf-edges-cannabis-margins-symptoms-caused-by-light-burn.jpg)
The leaves closest to the light may appear much more pale than the rest of the plant, and tips may turn yellow.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/12/light-burn-yellow-tips.jpg)Another example of yellow tips from light burn
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/12/light-stress-on-leaves-edges-tipped-up.jpg)
Sometimes light burn causes edges of leaves to turn up. If it goes on a long time, the leaves also start to become crispy and can even break off if you try to bend them
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/12/light-stressed-leaves-curled-up-on-edges.jpg)
You may noticed just the tallest colas getting droopy, which is sometimes a sign the light is too intense (though it could also be caused by[ root problems](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-root-problems) or [over/under watering](https://www.growweedeasy.com/drooping-over-water-cannabis-vs-under-water-cannabis))
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/12/droopy-colas-from-grow-light-being-too-close.jpg)
Light burn is often mistaken for a **[Nitrogen deficiency](https://www.growweedeasy.com/nitrogen-deficiency-cannabis)** which makes _wilting_ yellowstarts from the bottom of the plant and moves up, while light burn often is worse at the top of the plant.
Cannabis light burn usually affects the top leaves closest to the grow light
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/yellow-top-leaves-from-grow-light-being-too-close.jpg)
A [Nitrogen deficiency](https://www.growweedeasy.com/nitrogen-deficiency-cannabis) creates yellow leaves at the bottom of the plant as the Nitrogen is sucked out of the oldest leaves to feed the top of the plant. On the other hand, light burn produces yellow leaves at the **top** of the plant under the grow lights because the leaves have worked too hard and/or too long from the light being too close. The leaves aren’t able to keep up with regular plant processes.
Imagine sitting outside all day under a scorching sun, possibly for days on end. Even if you could handle it for a day or two, it might wear you down over days or weeks.
[How can there be too much light if there’s no heat? Read the full guide](https://www.growweedeasy.com/too-much-light)
It’s probably light burn if mostly just the leaves closest to the lights are turning yellow
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2014/04/light-bleached-leaves.jpg)
With cannabis plants that have light burn, your leaves can sometimes become **yellow** or **red/purple** , possibly with brown spotting, often with burnt tips/edges and margins that stay green. Other problems, like [nutrient problems](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-symptoms-pictures), can trigger or make the symptoms of light burn a lot worse. Leaves may also appear generally burnt in places when there’s too much light, especially when combined with heat or nutrient problems.
**Nutrient deficiencies make light burn worse!**
If you see light bleaching and unhealthy discoloration  _only_ on the parts of the plant directly under your grow light, or only on older leaves that are exposed to the light, it often means it’s too bright for your plants and you should move your grow lights further away! If your plant is also having other problems, it is much more likely to be affected by light burn. A healthy plant can withstand higher light levels than a sick plant.
If the lights are only**slightly** too close, maybe just an inch or a few cm, the yellowing from light burn may happen **slowly** over the course of days (or even weeks!) because leaves are dying _early_ instead of immediately. Because of that, light burn may first appear on somewhat **older leaves** , which can be confusing and make it hard to diagnose.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/06/light-stressed-plants.jpg)
This cannabis seedling is being burned by too-close LED grow lights
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/06/light-burned-leaves.jpg)
Another example of light burn from an LED grow light being kept too close to the plants
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/10/cannabis-led-light-burn-brown-spots-leaves.jpg)
These leaves of this LED-burnt plant started curling upwards
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/led-grow-light-burn-too-close-top-leaves-turn-yellow.jpg)
This cannabis seedling basically grew up _into_ the grow light! The heat from the bulb caused massive burning everywhere it touched. If a plant’s leaves directly touches the lights, it leaves “burns” from the heat of the bulbs.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/light-burned-top.jpg)
This plant was green and healthy through the vegetative stage under an LED grow light, but the leaves started dying soon after flowering started (even though that distance had been fine in the vegetative stage). The reason was the LED was too close. This is also very common with LED grow lights with just read and blue diodes, without any diodes in the green spectrum.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2014/04/leds-burnt-to-extreme.jpg)
These plants seem apparently healthy, but the top leaves keep getting lighter and lighter, in this case from a 600W HPS that was kept just under a foot (30cm) away. The leaves slowly turned yellow over the course of a few weeks, getting light burn even though the temperature was a comfortable 75°F (24°C).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/many-colas-canopy-due-to-marijuana-plant-training.jpg)
These yellow leaves were caused by an LED grow light that was too close. If you don’t realize it’s light burn, the symptoms are inexplicable!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2014/04/led-light-burn-top-leaves-yellow.jpg)
A mild case of marijuana light burn is often mistaken for a [nutrient deficiency](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-symptoms-pictures) or a [pH problem](https://www.growweedeasy.com/ph), but if you look closely, the symptoms are concentrated directly under the grow light.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/light-burn-top-leaves-yellowing.jpg)
**Light Bleached Cannabis Buds Sometimes Turn White**
This is how you get “albino” or white buds. Light bleaching is most common with high-power LEDs and HPS grow lights because these can be brighter than the sun. Basically, bud bleaching is what happens when buds get too much light, kinda like how hair can get bleached if you spend plenty of time in the sun. Except a “sun-burnt” bud is often less potent, and may have lost it’s “cannabis” smell!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/light-bleached-bud.jpg)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/cannabis-light-bleaching-white-bud-tip.jpg)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/light-bleached-cola.jpg)
Buds which have been bleached tend to be low potency or even have no potency (no available THC or other cannabinoids). Therefore you should avoid light-bleaching your plants at all costs!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2014/04/bleached-top-cannabis.jpg)
Sometimes light-bleached cannabis will get mis-labeled as “albino cannabis” or “white cannabis” but the truth is that the white color is not healthy, so this is not a desirable trait (even if it looks pretty cool).
**Most of the Time, Light-Burned Buds Appear Burnt**
Often though, light burned buds look like they’ve been burned.
LED-burnt cannabis buds – notice how all the tiny “sugar leaves” have turned yellow or brown
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/led-burnt-cannabis-bud-crispy.jpg)
In this case the LED-burn caused the leaves closest to the LED to turn red. Although the buds smoked pretty well anyway, they definitely weren’t as pretty as they could have been!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/led-burnt-cannabis-buds-turned-red.jpg)
The leaves too close to the LED grow light turned yellow and wilted. For some reason, cannabis plants seem a lot more prone to light burn after they start flowering.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/led-grow-light-burn-too-close-top-leaves-turn-yellow-wilted.jpg)
Another example of a bud that has light burn from a too-close LED
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/06/led-burnt-bud.jpg)
Light burned bud on top, healthy bud below
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/11/light-burn-bud-top-regular-bud-bottom.jpg)
**Solution:** If your marijuana plants are getting too much light, try removing some of the lights or moving your grow lights further away from the tops of the plants. If you can’t move the light further away, [bend your plants over](https://www.growweedeasy.com/lst) so the tops are further away or if your plant is still in the vegetative stage you might even consider [cutting off the top of the plant](https://www.growweedeasy.com/topping-fiming) to remove some of that height.
**Reduce power of grow lights and/or move them further away from your plant**  
([How far away do I keep grow lights from my plants?](https://www.growweedeasy.com/how-far-grow-lights))
It is unlikely for your plants to get “light-burned” from the sun when growing outdoors, and they definitely can’t accidentally grow into the sun. Outdoor plants can show signs of light stress if plants were used to shady conditions and moved into direct sunlight without time to get accustomed to the brigher light levels. It also may be possible in extreme high light conditions if the plant is unprotected but in general cannabis plants like a lot of light.
When making changes to your plant’s environment, it’s best to make changes relatively slowly if possible. For example when moving a cannabis plant from indoors or outdoors you might consider giving the plant some shade for a few days before moving it into full sunlight.
Sometimes **[heat stress](https://www.growweedeasy.com/heat-light-stress) **can look like light stress. When learning how to grow cannabis, it’s best to try to keep things at a [comfortable temperature](https://www.growweedeasy.com/temperature) at all times for optimal growth. If it’s too hot for you, it’s probably too hot for your plants. Outdoors, it’s a lot harder to control temperature, but there are steps you can take to protect your outdoor plants from the heat including supplementing with sea kelp, partially covering them and making sure they’re well watered.
[5 Ways to Deal with Heat When Growing Cannabis Indoors](https://www.growweedeasy.com/5-secrets-cannabis-heat-control)

## Plant Symptoms
  * [Bronze or brown patches](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/bronze-spots-or-patches)
  * [Brown or slimy roots](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/roots-are-brown-smelly-or-slimy)
  * [Brown or yellow leaf tips/edges](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/brown-yellow-or-burnt-edges-or-tips)
  * [Buds dying](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/buds-dying)
  * [Buds look odd](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/buds-look-odd)
  * [Bugs are visible](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/i-see-bugs)
  * [Curling or clawing leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/curling-or-clawing-leaves)
  * [Dark leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/dark-green)
  * [Drooping plant](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/drooping-plant)
  * [Holes in leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/holes-in-leaves)
  * [Mold or powder](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/mold-or-white-powder)
  * [Pink or purple on leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/pink-or-purple-on-leaves)
  * [Red stems](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/red-stems)
  * [Shiny or smooth leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/shiny-or-smooth-leaves)
  * [Spots or markings](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/spots-or-markings-on-leaves)
  * [Twisted growth](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/twisted-growth)
  * [Webbing](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/webbing)
  * [Wilting leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/wilting-leaves)
  * [Yellow between leaf veins](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/yellow-between-leaf-veins)
  * [Yellow leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/plant-problems-symptoms/yellow-leaves)

This page is part of our Plant Doctor series. You can use our tool to filter by symptom and help [diagnose your plant](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-symptoms-pictures).

GrowWeedEasy.com was [founded in 2010](https://www.growweedeasy.com/about) by Nebula Haze and Sirius Fourside. Featured in _High Times_ , Ed Rosenthal’s books, _Cannabis Business Times_ , and _Skunk Magazine_ , GrowWeedEasy.com is the **#1 free resource in the world** for learning how to grow cannabis at home. Our simple step-by-step tutorials make it easy for anyone to grow great weed. **[Sign up for our free newsletter](https://www.growweedeasy.com/message/news)** to get grow tips delivered every Sunday at 4:20 AM.
